text:
vi Sukra
Our fourth chart is that of Marie Antoinette, with planets
between the fourth and the eighth. Isolated Saturn and iso
lated Mars are very striking here. Further Mercury is alone in
the sixth. Mercury indicates a way of acting in which the
supremacy of idea over material things is made personal. Here
action proceeds with the awareness ofthe various factors of
experience and ordering them. Mercury unravels the pattern
of mind. When Mercury is a morning star, the mind of the
native will be eager. When this distance is more than fourteen
degrees, the mind is completely untramelled and free to exam
ine everything, and free to deal everything as he likes. Such
are the cases of Marie Antoinette and of our fifth chart which

Directions: Preliminaries 97
belongs to Trotsky. Such too was the case with Queen Victo
ria. All these were exceptionally alert to their responsibilities
and were interested in all these things with a child-like in
genuousness.
